questions.question: Which of the following sources mentions that Prithviraj Chauhan ruled Ajmer even after his defeat in the second battle of Tarain?
questions.explanation: It can be considered that Prithviraj was allowed to rule over Ajmer for some time even after his defeat in the second battle of Tarain as the coins pertaining to this period bear legend “Prithvirajadeva” on one side and the words ”Sri Muhammad Sam” on the other.

questions.question: Which of the following military general of Alauddin led the Khalji dynasty invasions to South India?
questions.explanation: Alauddin married Kamla Bai who was the wife of Rai Karan, the ruler of Gujarat and acquired Malik Kafur who was an eunuch, who later led the Khalji dynasty invasions to South India. Malik Kafur also became reason of death of Alauddin.

questions.question: Which of the following was renamed Khizrabad after the name of Alauddin’s son Khizr Khan?
questions.explanation: Chittor was renamed Khizrabad after the name of Alauddin’s son named Khizr Khan. After the conquest of Chittor Alauddin had to return back quickly to Delhi as the Mongol army was advancing towards Delhi.

questions.question: Which of the following sultans founded the city of Jaunpur?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah Tughluq founded the city of Jaunpur which he named after his cousin Jauna or Juna Khan, i.e. Muhammad bin Tughluq. Jaunpur developed into a strong military force during the rule of Sharqi dynasty, and the Delhi Sultanate was often threatened by this power.

questions.question: Malwa was annexed to the Delhi Sultanate by which of the following rulers?
questions.explanation: Ala-ud-din Khalji annexed the state of Malwa and included it in the Delhi Sultanate in 1305. It continued to be under the authority of Delhi Sultanate till 1398 when it became independent.

questions.question: The second Guhila dynasty of Chittor was established by which of the following kings?
questions.explanation: After the defeat of Chittorgarh by the armies of Sultan Alauddin Khalji, Mewar had become relatively insignificant. Later in 1335 CE, Rana Hammira established the Second Guhila dynasty of Chittor.

questions.question: Which of the following started the festival of ‘Nauroz’?
questions.explanation: The sultans of the Delhi Sultanate built lofty palaces, gave expensive gifts to the nobility and others on occasions such as the sultan’s birthday, Nauroz which was the Persian New Year and coronation ceremonies. Nauroz was introduced by Balban.

questions.question: Vittalaswamy and Hazara Ramaswamy temples at Vijayanagar were built by which of the following?
questions.explanation: Krishna Deva Raya built some fine stone temples. The most famous among them are Vittalaswamy and Hazara Ramaswamy temples at Vijayanagar. He also got many temples repaired and added impressive gopurams or gateways to many important South Indian temples.

questions.question: Vittalaswamy and Hazara Ramaswamy temples were built in rule of which Vijayanagara empire?
questions.explanation: Vittalaswamy and Hazara Ramaswamy temples built by Krishna Deva Raya. Hazara Ramaswamy temple adorned with sculptures illustrates the main events from the Ramayana. Vittalaswami temple is a portrays magnificence in floral sculpture.

questions.question: Which of the following king bestowed Mohammed Adil Shah with the title of Shah?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Adil Shah signed a peace treaty with Shah Jahan in 1636 and was recognized as sovereign in 1648. He built the Gol Gumbaz, known for its large dome, and promoted Deccan art and culture, making Bijapur a center for Persian and Dakhni literature.
